global.title: Connect-on-Swarm data spans four distinct storage layers with different durability
global.content:
Connect-on-Swarm data is distributed across four layers with distinct durability properties:

**Layer 1 — PGlite (browser, ephemeral)**: Operations, documents, relationships, keyframes. Survives page refresh via IndexedDB, lost on "Clear site data", new device, or new browser.

**Layer 2 — Swarm /bytes (immutable, permanent until stamp expires)**: Encrypted operation batches and manifest payloads. Content-addressed (uploading the same data twice produces the same reference), replicated across the neighborhood. Lost only when stamps expire and chunks are evicted from reserves.

**Layer 3 — Swarm feeds (mutable pointers)**: 64-char hex references pointing to /bytes data. Each feed index is write-once; the feed as a whole is append-only. One feed per topic. Same durability as /bytes.

**Layer 4 — IndexedDB caches (browser, ephemeral helpers)**: Manifest index (docId -> latest /bytes ref, for bee dev mode), pending ops buffer, wallet-derived signer key cache. Survives page refresh, lost on "Clear site data".

Each layer has a specific role: PGlite is the hot read path, /bytes is the durable payload store, feeds are the mutable lookup, and IndexedDB accelerates repeated lookups.
global.description: Data lives in four layers: PGlite (ephemeral), Swarm /bytes (immutable), Swarm feeds (append-only pointers), and IndexedDB caches — each with different loss conditions.
